mentions.note: Chapel Trust long position since 2008; stock $176→$209 snapback on no news shows clearing price reached. Pain of selling low > conviction to exit; hold for regret avoidance.
Software strength resuming; AI integration and enterprise platform demand offsetting earlier concerns.

Record $11B Q1 revenue (+14% YoY), Agentforce >$1B revenue, million-dollar deals at peak, NRR 126%. $27B buyback at 12x earnings shows management conviction. SaaS apocalypse narrative false; Anthropic and OpenAI using Slack as core infrastructure; Cramer owns for charitable trust.
Enterprise software vendor facing disruption from AI; executives questioning ROI of expensive per-user licensing when AI agents can replace work.
Enterprise software rotation stock, up 3.5% today; move feels like short squeeze; history shows torrential selling after outperformance.
Cloud software company with honest, credible CEO Marc Benioff; during 2008 Great Recession, Benioff said cloud was future and was right; rewards faithful shareholders.
Market hates software across the board (Palantir, ServiceNow, Salesforce, Workday, Adobe). Cramer notes it's one of his smallest positions; won't push software plays.
Marc Benioff's 2030 AI thesis intact; stock incredibly cheap at near cost basis; hold for long-term vision; don't sell at current prices.
$50B buyback (1/3 of float at current levels) announced; up 4.7% today but down 34% YTD; software index bounce but structural AI concerns persist.
Nico's position; uncertain on CEO Marc Benioff's ability to build data center business; software sector under pressure; hard own going forward.
Enterprise software leader down nearly 3%; Cramer notes potential contrarian opportunity as Aru simulation shows 2x enterprise confidence vs. new AI startups.
Down 4% on AI worker-displacement narrative; SaaS model considered guilty until proven innocent despite best-in-class AI agents and Slack integration.
Agent Force and Slack AI transformation underpinned by record cash flow and $50B buyback; stock at $186.67 vastly undervalues software renaissance narrative.
Legacy enterprise software under disruption from AI upstarts; losing relevance despite remaining profitable; margin pressure inevitable.
Ron asks long-term hold; Cramer respects CEO Bill McDermott, won't bet against him; stock represents actual value at current prices; bond offering strength evident.
Enterprise software rebound on Broadcom strength; company far from out of business despite AI pressures.
Widley considered not-great quarter but stock U-turned from -$8 to rebound $181→$196; enterprise software multiple compression may be over.

Dropped $10 initially to $182, rallied to $199 (+$7.82) on $50B buyback announcement. Agent Force ($800M ARR, +169% YoY) is real growth driver. CEO Benioff fired up; overcome SaaS apocalypse skepticism. Don't underestimate this company.
Rallied $7.82 to $199 on $50B buyback and excellent numbers; AgentForce momentum real; CEO confident and willing to back conviction.
Enterprise software under threat from Anthropic AI; clients shortening contracts; stock face structural headwinds from AI-driven code generation and automation capabilities.
Enterprise software king with AI integration but down 33% YTD; trades under 15x earnings—valuation may become compelling if earnings hold despite AI displacement fears.
Own for Travel Trust; down 43%, 14x earnings (lowest multiple in history including 2008); 12% growth slightly slower than S&P; near bottom; good entry point.
Enterprise software provider down 7% on AI substitution fears; multiple compression despite positive earnings; unknown floor for valuation.
Down 20% in January; multiple compressed from 30x to 16x forward earnings; Cramer Trust owns but multiple compression story dominates; could have made same argument a month ago.
Same multiple compression dynamic as ServiceNow. Per-user SaaS model vulnerability to AI employee displacement. Market unforgiving despite strong business. Wait for multiple stabilization.
Won $5.6B 10-year Army procurement contract but stock fell 87 cents; SaaS segment in doghouse from AI competitive threat despite great news.
Enterprise software suffering extreme P/E multiple shrinkage on irrational AI disruption fears despite no earnings misses; momentum money fleeing to storage; psychological, not fundamental.
Enterprise software weakness; stock not doing well on the charts.
Slammed over AI displacement fears; market irrationally treats AI as existential threat to software companies.
High-quality cloud software company that became too expensive in late 2021 rally; should have been trimmed into strength; good company but better buying opportunity after pullbacks.
2021 cloud-software bull run was followed by severe drawdown post-Fed tightening; selling strength would have protected gains despite solid fundamentals.
